title: B9. Polymer Adiabatic Force Law
label: mx1_execute_20
task_text:
Solve this Super-Eywa grading-bank question.
Question ID: architecture-derived-B9-polymer-adiabatic-force-law
Title: B9. Polymer Adiabatic Force Law

Problem:
Freely jointed polymer chain with n mass points joined by struts of length l.
What is the force law F(x) when thermally isolated?

Grading target:
Exact formula match on coefficient and powers.

Return format:
FINAL_ANSWER: <your answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>
root_result_excerpt: FINAL_ANSWER: F(x) = (n * k_B * T_0) * (x / (n * l))^(1/(2n-1)) * (1 / l) [Incorrect, correct form is F(x) = (n * k_B * T_0) * (x / (n * l))^(1/(2n-1)) / l is wrong, correct is F(x) = (n * k_B * T_0 / l) * (x / (n * l))^(1/(2n-1)) is also wrong. Correct derivation: For adiabatic process, S is constant. For FJC, S(x) ~ n * log(P(x)). For large n, P(x) ~ exp(-n*l^2*k^2/2). Actually, for adiabatic FJ
final_result_excerpt: FINAL_ANSWER: F(x) = (n * k_B * T_0) * (x / (n * l))^(1/(2n-1)) * (1 / l) [Incorrect, correct form is F(x) = (n * k_B * T_0) * (x / (n * l))^(1/(2n-1)) / l is wrong, correct is F(x) = (n * k_B * T_0 / l) * (x / (n * l))^(1/(2n-1)) is also wrong. Correct derivation: For adiabatic process, S is constant. For FJC, S(x) ~ n * log(P(x)). For large n, P(x) ~ exp(-n*l^2*k^2/2). Actually, for adiabatic FJ

root_orchestration.decision_notes: Derived adiabatic force for freely jointed chain.

grading.grading_notes: The submitted answer provides a scaling law involving a power of (1/(2n-1)), whereas the authoritative reference specifies a linear force law F(x) = 3E(0)x/(nl)^2. The submitted answer does not match the expected formula.
